es.meaning: At all costs; no matter what.
es.exampleTranslation: She wants to win at all costs.

it.meaning: At all costs; no matter what.
it.example: Vuole vincere a tutti i costi.
it.exampleTranslation: She wants to win at all costs.

bridge.note: Same vow, different arithmetic: Italian pays all the costs (plural), Spanish pays at all cost (singular) — and Spanish costa here is an old word for expense, not the seaside.

interference.es_to_it: Spanish singular pulls 'a tutto il costo' — Italian counts the costs: a tutti i costi, plural.
interference.it_to_es: Italian plural pulls 'a todas las costas' — Spanish keeps one indivisible costa (and costa here is not the coast).

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
